title: bash-classify command definition
description: Schema for YAML command database files used by bash-classify

oneOf.description: Alias file: points at another command, mutually exclusive with classification-bearing fields

alias_of.description: Name of another command in the database whose definition should be used verbatim. Mutually exclusive with classification/risk/subcommands/options/global_options/subcommand_mode/delegates_to/strict.

risk.description: Explicit risk level override for this command (default derived from classification)

strict.description: If true (default), unrecognized options yield UNKNOWN

global_options.description: Options that appear before subcommands and are stripped before matching

options.description: Options that affect classification of this command

Classification.description: Classification level for a command or option override

Risk.description: Risk level for a command or option override

SubcommandMode.description: How subcommands are matched: hierarchical (chained tree walk, default) or match_all (each positional arg matched independently)

separator.description: For after_separator mode: the token that separates wrapper args from inner args

terminator.description: For terminated_argv mode: the token that ends the inner argv

flag.description: For flag_value_is_expression mode: which flag's value to parse

strip_assignments.description: For rest_are_argv mode: strip leading KEY=VALUE tokens before the inner command

min_classification.description: Floor classification for the inner command

takes_value.description: Whether the option consumes the next token as its value

overrides.description: When this option is present, override the classification to this level

risk.description: When this option is present, override the risk level

captures_directory.description: The value of this option is a working directory

OptionsMap.description: Map of option name to option definition

risk.description: Explicit risk level override for this subcommand (default derived from classification)

strict.description: If true (default), unrecognized options yield UNKNOWN

SubcommandsMap.description: Map of subcommand name to subcommand definition
